promptImprovement.improvePromptDescription: Opens the prompt in the Playground for improvement
promptImprovement.generatePlaceholder: What do you want your AI to do?

promptImprovement.initialPromptDescription: This is your initial prompt. Optional instructions can guide improvements, or we'll optimize it automatically.
promptImprovement.instructionsDescription: Write your instructions in plain language. We'll turn them into a best-practice prompt that you can review and iterate on.
promptImprovement.generatedPromptDescription: This is your generated prompt, created with the selected model ({{modelDisplayName}}) and parameters. It's editable.
promptImprovement.generatedPromptPlaceholder: The generated prompt will be shown here, ready for you to review and edit
promptImprovement.promptCutOff: The generated prompt was cut off due to token limits. Please try increasing the max_tokens setting in the model configuration or use a shorter instruction.
promptImprovement.noContentReturned: The model did not return any content. Please try again or adjust your instructions.

chatPromptRawView.editRawJsonDescription: Edit the raw JSON representation of chat messages. Must be a valid JSON array with at least one object. Each object must have a "role" field (system/user/assistant) and a "content" field (string or array of objects).

llmPromptMessageActions.loadPromptDescription: You have unsaved changes in your message field. Loading a new prompt will overwrite them with the prompt's content. This action cannot be undone.

For each one, select a field from a recent trace to map it — including image fields like input.image_url or output.image_base64. These mappings auto-fill the variables during rule execution.

ollamaProviderDetails.urlMustEndWithDescription: URL must end with /v1 for OpenAI API compatibility. For local Ollama: http://localhost:11434/v1. For remote: http://ollama-test:11434/v1 or your server URL with /v1 suffix.

sharedModelConfigs.temperatureTooltip: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive.
sharedModelConfigs.temperatureReasoningTooltip: Reasoning models require temperature = 1.0. This setting controls randomness in completions.
sharedModelConfigs.maxOutputTokensTooltip: The maximum number of tokens to generate shared between the prompt and completion. The exact limit varies by model. (One token is roughly 4 characters for standard English text).
sharedModelConfigs.topPTooltip: Controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered
sharedModelConfigs.throttlingTooltip: Minimum time in seconds between consecutive requests to avoid rate limiting
sharedModelConfigs.maxConcurrentRequestsTooltip: Maximum number of requests that can run simultaneously. Set to 1 for sequential execution, higher values for parallel processing
sharedModelConfigs.anthropicTemperatureTooltip: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive. Note: Anthropic models require using either Temperature OR Top P, not both.
sharedModelConfigs.anthropicTopPTooltip: Controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered. Note: Anthropic models require using either Temperature OR Top P, not both.

anthropicModelConfigs.thinkingEffortDescription: Controls how much effort Claude puts into thinking before responding. Higher effort produces more thorough analysis but takes longer. Uses adaptive thinking mode.

openAIModelConfigs.reasoningEffortDescription: Controls how much effort the model puts into reasoning before responding. Higher effort may result in more thoughtful but slower responses.
openAIModelConfigs.frequencyPenaltyTooltip: How much to penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far. Decreases the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim
openAIModelConfigs.presencePenaltyTooltip: How much to penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far. Increases the model's likelihood to talk about new topics

geminiModelConfigs.thinkingLevelDescription: Controls the depth of reasoning the model performs before responding. Higher thinking level may result in more thorough but slower responses.

vertexAIModelConfigs.thinkingLevelDescription: Controls the depth of reasoning the model performs before responding. Higher thinking level may result in more thorough but slower responses.

customModelConfigs.temperatureTooltip: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive.
customModelConfigs.maxOutputTokensTooltip: The maximum number of tokens to generate shared between the prompt and completion. The exact limit varies by model. (One token is roughly 4 characters for standard English text).
customModelConfigs.topPTooltip: Controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered
customModelConfigs.frequencyPenaltyTooltip: How much to penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far. Decreases the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim
customModelConfigs.presencePenaltyTooltip: How much to penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far. Increases the model's likelihood to talk about new topics

openRouterModelConfigs.maxTokensTooltip: The maximum number of tokens to generate shared between the prompt and completion. The exact limit varies by model. (One token is roughly 4 characters for standard English text).
openRouterModelConfigs.topKTooltip: This limits the model's choice of tokens at each step, making it choose from a smaller set. A value of 1 means the model will always pick the most likely next token, leading to predictable results. By default this setting is disabled, making the model to consider all choices.
openRouterModelConfigs.frequencyPenaltyTooltip: This setting aims to control the repetition of tokens based on how often they appear in the input. It tries to use less frequently those tokens that appear more in the input, proportional to how frequently they occur. Token penalty scales with the number of occurrences. Negative values will encourage token reuse.
openRouterModelConfigs.presencePenaltyTooltip: Adjusts how often the model repeats specific tokens already used in the input. Higher values make such repetition less likely, while negative values do the opposite. Token penalty does not scale with the number of occurrences. Negative values will encourage token reuse.
openRouterModelConfigs.repetitionPenaltyTooltip: Helps to reduce the repetition of tokens from the input. A higher value makes the model less likely to repeat tokens, but too high a value can make the output less coherent (often with run-on sentences that lack small words). Token penalty scales based on original token's probability.
openRouterModelConfigs.minPTooltip: Represents the minimum probability for a token to be considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. (The value changes depending on the confidence level of the most probable token.) If your Min-P is set to 0.1, that means it will only allow for tokens that are at least 1/10th as probable as the best possible option.
openRouterModelConfigs.topATooltip: Consider only the top tokens with "sufficiently high" probabilities based on the probability of the most likely token. Think of it like a dynamic Top-P. A lower Top-A value focuses the choices based on the highest probability token but with a narrower scope. A higher Top-A value does not necessarily affect the creativity of the output, but rather refines the filtering process based on the maximum probability.
